key_insight: PG-KINN combines a Kolmogorov-Arnold Network trial space with a Petrov-Galerkin weak form to improve physics-informed PDE solving beyond standard MLP and strong-form approaches.
engineering_relevance: This makes the method more suitable for solid mechanics problems with nonlinear constitutive laws, singularities, complex geometries, and inverse parameter identification.
method: KAN as the trial/surrogate space with spline-based learnable activations
Independent compactly supported piecewise-polynomial test functions in a Petrov-Galerkin weak form
Gauss-Legendre quadrature with integration by parts to reduce derivative order and improve conditioning
evidence_signal: The abstract reports consistent gains over legacy MLP baselines and state-of-the-art KAN-based strong/energy/inverse formulations across benchmarks including crack singularities, stress concentration, Neo-Hookean hyperelasticity, inverse media identification, and complex geometries.
watch_item: Performance depends on constructing suitable localized test functions and quadrature, so implementation complexity and mesh/element design may affect robustness.
paper_title: PG-KINN: A Physics-Informed Petrov-Galerkin Kolmogorov-Arnold Network for Solving Forward and Inverse PDEs
